validation.consistency_check: archive_total == DB COUNT(*) WHERE polled_at <= archive_max : PASS (13155461 == 13155461). The DB is live (rows added during the run); SQLite gave the streamed SELECT a consistent snapshot as of query-start (13:01:31), so this snapshot-bounded check is the correct invariant rather than a raw COUNT(*) on a growing table.

incremental_validation_run.note: ran --mode incremental once to validate the cron's exact code path (stream-merge append) before committing

deviations_from_drafted_producer.memory_safety: The originally-drafted write_monthly_partitions buffered an entire month in a Python list before flushing (flush only on month change) AND used read-existing+concat+rewrite for appends. The source has a ~10M-row month (2026-05); on the VPS (~1.47 GB free RAM) that buffer would have exceeded the 1.4 GB STOP gate and OOM'd, contradicting the prompt's '<500 MB peak' / 'memory-safe streaming' intent. Replaced with bounded-batch SQLite streaming straight through a per-month pyarrow ParquetWriter; incremental appends stream-merge the existing file's row groups one at a time then atomic-rename. Output contract unchanged (same by_month/YYYY-MM.parquet layout, same 12-col schema, byte-faithful values). Measured peak RSS: 314 MB (initial), 253 MB (incremental).
deviations_from_drafted_producer.high_water_mark_correctness: The drafted code set last_archived_ts from a separate 'SELECT MAX(polled_at)' taken after the stream. On a live DB that MAX() races ahead of the streamed snapshot (recorded 13:09:49 vs true archived max 13:01:31), which would leave the (13:01:31, 13:09:49] window — 73,734 rows — permanently un-archivable by incremental runs (polled_at > last_archived_ts). Fixed: high-water-mark is now the max polled_at actually written. state.json was reconciled post-hoc to the true archived max for the initial run.
